rust.framework_notes: Hexagonal / clean architecture: domain → application → port-interfaces → adapter (postgres, redis, http, cache) + cross-cutting (logging, error, health, event-sourcing, state-machine, policy-engine, contracts, config-core, ffi_utils)

typescript.tooling: bun + @types/bun 1.3.13; bun.lock present at repo root

ci.billing_status: blocked org-wide (per STATUS.md); workflows enrolled but awaiting live runs

evidence: README.md (9457B, pinned-refs block, badges, 17-crate table, quick-start with git-deps)

19 workflows: alert-sync-issues, cargo-audit, cargo-deny, cargo-machete, cargo-semver-checks, ci, codeql, docs-deploy, legacy-tooling-gate, release, reusable/, reusable-release-drafter, scorecard, secrets-scan, security-guard-hook-audit, self-merge-gate, tag-automation, trufflehog, vitepress-pages
.github/dependabot.yml (cargo + gomod + pip + npm, daily) + renovate.json5 (cargo + github-actions, weekly)

dup_exposure.rationale: phenoShared is the org-wide convergence hub. The 'dup_exposure' lists pheno-* libs that this repo could itself consume (replace in-tree or git-only deps) and adjacent libs it could share config/build recipes with.

candidates.use_case: Replace ad-hoc tokio::mpsc in phenotype-application with canonical bus; current phenotype-bus is already git-pulled from HexaKit
Propagate request IDs across domain/application crates; surfaces in error-envelope context for trace correlation
Share deny.toml + cargo-deny + cargo-audit policy with the org baseline; only one deny.toml currently in repo root
Service-registry contract that phenotype-health (multi-checker compose) and phenotype-port-interfaces (outbound) could share
Post-effect / middleware runner for #[instrument] wrappers on port traits, replacing manual layer stacking
Shared icon set for VitePress docs / reference pages
Journey primitives to gate the existing FR-WS-XXX functional requirements
Roll justfile + Makefile + Taskfile.yml + mise.toml under one shared tooling lib; this repo has 4 separate task-runner files today
Expose phenotype-* skills from the workspace as manifest entries
General hex toolkit; phenotype-error-core and phenotype-bus were originally pulled from it — risk of drift between this repo and HexaKit HEAD
Observability contract overlap with phenotype-logging + phenotype-port-interfaces/outbound/logger
AI/LLM utility surface that phenotype-port-interfaces/outbound could model an `LLMPort` against
Data-pipeline traits reusable in phenotype-event-sourcing adapters
Design tokens for the VitePress docs site (docs/ directory)
Build / codegen recipes that phenotype-config-core could host
Research / experiment harness; the pheno-shared TS surface overlaps the Python research side
VitePress pages host lives here; phenodocs could own the doc theme once
TypeScript counterpart of @phenotype/{types,errors,ids} exists in a sibling repo; risk of two TS-shared repos diverging

sota_gaps: phenotype-event-sourcing has pre-existing compilation errors — phenotype-application/Cargo.toml:23 explicitly comments the path-dep out: '# NOTE: phenotype-event-sourcing has pre-existing compilation errors'. The whole event-sourcing crate is silently absent from the application layer.

Working-tree churn on the audited branch: feat/phenoshared-build-default-http-client-20260608 has ~50 modified files + 1 untracked (crates/phenotype-error-core/src/error_reporter.rs) on top of HEAD 172c3f5b; no commit has captured the in-flight work since 2026-06-08 18:24:34

Public error enums in phenotype-error-core/layered.rs (ApiError, ConfigError, DomainError, RepositoryError, StorageError) are not marked #[non_exhaustive] — consumers cannot compile against future variants added by the 0.x → 1.x migration
No property-based testing (proptest / quickcheck) for value-object invariants despite FR-DOM-002 requiring constructor validation; only hand-rolled #[test] cases exist
No fuzz harnesses for deserialization paths: phenotype-event-sourcing/event.rs, phenotype-contracts, phenotype-error-core/envelope.rs all deserialize untrusted input without cargo-fuzz coverage

Outbound port traits still use the #[async_trait] macro (phenotype-port-interfaces/outbound/*) rather than native async-fn-in-traits stabilized in Rust 1.75 — adds a transitive dep and breaks dyn-compat

phenotype-test-support is missing from the workspace (Cargo.toml:9-27 lists 17 members; commit fb74c41 'add phenotype-test-support' is on chore/remove-niche-subcrates but the crate is absent from HEAD 172c3f5)

No crates.io publish pipeline: README 'Quick Start' tells consumers to add crates as git deps (`{ git = "..." }`) — no semver, no `cargo publish` workflow, no release-plz / release-please automation (cliff.toml exists but is unused)
No cargo-semver-checks baseline checked into main; the cargo-semver-checks.yml workflow runs ad-hoc with no persisted API surface snapshot
TS packages (@phenotype/types, @phenotype/errors, @phenotype/ids) have no dedicated CI workflow; only vitepress-pages.yml touches the packages/ directory — typecheck/test not gated
CHANGELOG drift: workspace.package.version = 0.1.0, VERSION = 0.1.0, CHANGELOG last entry = [0.3.0] - 2026-03-28; 0.4.0 / 0.5.0 work since March is unrecorded
phenotype-nanovms-client is a thin wrapper that 'shells out to the ops CLI' (per crate description) rather than a native Rust SDK — process-spawn latency, no streaming, no shared type model
No SBOM emission: no cargo-cyclonedx, no cargo-metadata export step in ci.yml or release.yml; consumers cannot generate a signed CycloneDX for compliance
No OpenTelemetry exporter wiring in phenotype-logging (init_tracing exists but writes to stdout only — no OTLP layer, no exemplars, no log/metric correlation)
phenotype-cache-adapter advertises 'two-tier LRU + DashMap with TTL' (per AGENTS.md) but only moka+Lru+parking_lot+ttl in code — no cardinality cap, no LFU tier, no remote-cache backend
phenotype-state-machine has adapters/ + application/ + domain/ + prelude.rs but no macro / derive(StateMachine) for declarative FSMs — boilerplate-heavy for callers
phenotype-health/checkers.rs is missing a /ready vs /live split (k8s-style probes) and an aggregated-composition primitive beyond project.rs
phenotype-config-core has no remote-source loader (consul/etcd/vault/S3) — config is file/env only
phenotype-contracts is 'async trait contracts' but the TS wire schema in @phenotype/errors is hand-maintained, not generated from Rust types (no ts-rs / specta bridge)
convergence_rationale: phenoShared is the explicit org-wide convergence point for shared infrastructure. It hosts 17 Rust crates spanning domain → application → port → adapter layers plus 3 TypeScript packages (@phenotype/{types,errors,ids}) and a VitePress docs site in 5 locales. The stated project goal (per AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md) is to extract shared infrastructure from across the Phenotype polyrepo into one consumable hub. It is a pure convergence artifact — no deployable binary, no runtime product — built to be consumed by every other Pheno repo. The 'convergence' classification fits better than 'multi-stack' because the polyglot surface (Rust + TS + docs) is in service of a single library hub, not multiple deployable products.

action_items.title: Commit the in-flight working-tree churn on feat/phenoshared-build-default-http-client-20260608
action_items.why: Source branch has 50+ modified files + 1 untracked (phenotype-error-core/src/error_reporter.rs) since 2026-06-08 18:24:34; an audit diff is meaningless while the worktree is dirty. Commit (or stash) first, then re-audit. Likely 2-3 logical commits (http-client, error-reporter, domain updates).

action_items.title: Re-enable phenotype-event-sourcing in phenotype-application/Cargo.toml:23 and ship a fix release
action_items.why: The path-dep is commented out with 'pre-existing compilation errors' — this silently disables the event-sourcing integration the application layer was designed around (see FUNCTIONAL_REQUIREMENTS FR-EVT-*). Trivial reproduction: `cargo build -p phenotype-application` succeeds, but `cargo build -p phenotype-application --features event-sourcing` (or similar) would fail. Diagnose, patch, uncomment, cut 0.2.0.

action_items.title: Add #[non_exhaustive] to public error enums + cut a 0.2.x → 0.3.x semver release
action_items.why: phenotype-error-core/layered.rs is at 0.2.0 and exposes 5 enum types (ApiError, ConfigError, DomainError, RepositoryError, StorageError) without #[non_exhaustive]. Every downstream consumer compiles against a closed variant set. Adding the attribute is one-line per enum; the semver bump communicates the breaking change. This unblocks additive variants in 0.4+.

action_items.title: Add proptest coverage for value-object invariants (FR-DOM-002) and a workspace fuzz harness
action_items.why: FR-DOM-002 requires constructor validation; the current tests are hand-rolled happy/sad paths. proptest would explore the full boundary space for AgentId / TaskId / WorkflowId / Timestamp / etc. and surface invariant violations. Pair with cargo-fuzz on the three deserialization paths (event-sourcing/event.rs, contracts, error-core/envelope.rs) — untrusted input from event-sourced streams and inbound HTTP makes this SOTA, not nice-to-have.

action_items.title: Add crates.io publish + release-plz automation (move consumers off git-only deps)
action_items.why: README 'Quick Start' tells every consumer to use `{ git = "https://github.com/KooshaPari/phenoShared" }`. This pins every consumer to whatever HEAD exists at build time with no semver, no tag, no API guarantee. A release-plz (or release-please) workflow + first crates.io publish of all 17 crates removes the cross-repo coupling, makes `cargo update` useful, and unblocks CI in repos that need a deterministic dep source. Pairs with the existing cliff.toml.
